ground_truth.title: How Much Does Outpatient Mental Health Therapy and Treatment Cost Without Health Insurance Coverage Near Me - A Mission For Michael
ground_truth.main_content: An outpatient mental health counseling, psychotherapy, and treatment program provides you with professional support while allowing you to live at home and maintain your daily routine. Unlike inpatient programs, where you stay at a facility, outpatient programs offer flexibility, letting you attend sessions during the day or evening based on your schedule. These programs are designed to help you manage diagnoses like depression, anxiety, and PTSD. You'll engage in various therapeutic activities, like Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT), Dialectical Behavior Therapy (DBT), Interpersonal Therapy (IPT), talk therapy, psychodynamic therapy, family therapy, group therapy, individual therapy, and skill-building exercises tailored to your needs. The average cost of mental health disorder treatment in the country varies widely, typically ranging from $100 to $500 per session for outpatient therapy. The total of your treatment charges depends on factors such as the type and duration of treatment and your location. Inpatient programs can be more expensive, often costing between $500 and $2,000 per day. Insurance coverage, payment plans, and sliding scale fees can help reduce your out-of-pocket expenses. We offer multiple ways to cover the cost of your outpatient treatment expenses. You can use your health insurance, set up payment plans, or take advantage of sliding-scale fees if you're on a limited income.

To verify your insurance and discuss the average cost of outpatient mental health treatment, contact us at (866) 478-4383.
